top_risks: CLIENT-REJECT (sev 9): The approved key-art (approved_keyart) shows TWO MALE silhouettes as hero and foil, but the mood_reference supplied is a SINGLE FEMALE athlete — the cast gender and figure count are completely mismatched, making it impossible to source matching Adobe Stock stand-ins that satisfy both the locked composition and the lighting reference simultaneously without client clarification. I will not approve work that starts from contradictory inputs.

AGENT CHOKE on 'approved_keyart': The pipeline's first critical action is image_select_subject + image_remove_background on the hero figure, but approved_keyart is a 1024x1536 flattened JPG with two deeply merged, backlit silhouettes sharing overlapping edges against a blown-out dusk sky — subject selection will either grab both figures as one blob or fail to isolate either cleanly, immediately corrupting every downstream composite step before a single Stock plate is even licensed.
Executability 6/10: typography_sample left-edge crop means image_vectorize produces a broken logo vector — document_render_layout will embed defective title lockup
